title_en: The Superyacht, the Billionaire, and a Wildly Improbable Disaster at Sea
title_zh: 游艇、亿万富翁与一次惊人的海上灾难
rubric_en: The last night of tech mogul Mike Lynch’s life has become fodder for conspiracy theories. For the first time, the whole story can be told.
rubric_zh: 科技巨头迈克·林奇生命中的最后一夜已成为阴谋论的素材。如今，完整的故事终于可以讲述出来了。

paragraphs.en: In the predawn hours of August 19, 2024, bolts of lightning began to fork through the purple-black clouds above the Mediterranean. From the rail of a 184-foot vessel, a 22-year-old named Matthew Griffiths took out his phone to record a video. The British deckhand was just a week and a half into his first official yacht job, and he wasn’t on just any boat. The yacht, the $40 million Bayesian, was a star of the superyacht world, considered to be a feat of minimal design and precision engineering. As thunder rolled toward the anchored vessel, Griffiths set the video to AC/DC’s “Thunderstruck” and posted it to Instagram. It was 3:55 am.
paragraphs.zh: 在2024年8月19日的黎明前时分，闪电在地中海上空的紫黑色乌云中交织闪烁。英国人马修·格里菲斯（Matthew Griffiths）站在一艘长184英尺的游艇栏杆旁，拿出手机录制视频。这个22岁少年刚上任一周半的第一份正式游艇工作，而且他所在的船也非同一般。那艘价值4,000万美元的“Bayesian”号是超级游艇圈的明星，因其极简设计与精密工程而被视为杰作。当雷声朝着停泊着的船只滚来时，格里菲斯把视频配上了AC/DC的《Thunderstruck》，并上传到了Instagram。时间是凌晨3:55。

paragraphs.en: In the video, the Bayesian’s aluminum mast, one of the tallest in the world, is briefly visible against the roiling sky.
paragraphs.zh: 在视频里，Bayesian号那根世界上最高的铝制桅杆之一短暂地映衬在翻滚的天空背景中。

paragraphs.en: Below deck, the yacht’s owner, Michael Lynch, had every reason to be sleeping soundly. The boat trip had been organized as a celebration. Months earlier, Lynch had walked out of a San Francisco federal courthouse a free man, acquitted of all charges in one of the largest fraud cases in Silicon Valley history.
paragraphs.zh: 在船下层，游艇主人迈克尔·林奇（Michael Lynch）理应是睡得很沉的。整趟航程本来就是为庆祝而安排的。几个月前，林奇从旧金山的一座联邦法院走出来，成为自由之人，在硅谷历史上规模最大的诈骗案件之一中被无罪释放。

paragraphs.en: Lynch had built his fortune on understanding probability, on turning the unlikely into the possible. He had named his yacht Bayesian in honor of the statistical theorem that made him a billionaire, after the sale, in 2011, of his company Autonomy. The British tech giant sold software that could find meaningful signals amid the flood of unstructured data in emails, videos, and phone calls, but it would be better known as the company that allegedly defrauded, and nearly destroyed, Hewlett-Packard.
paragraphs.zh: 林奇靠对概率的理解致富，将看似不可能的事情变成了可能。他在2011年出售公司Autonomy后，以自己致富的统计定理“贝叶斯（Bayes）”命名了这艘游艇。英国这家科技巨头出售的软件，能够在电子邮件、视频和电话通话等海量非结构化数据中寻找有意义的信号；但后来它更广为人知的是曾被指控实施欺诈，几乎把惠普（Hewlett-Packard）拖入毁灭边缘。

paragraphs.en: The cabins aboard the Bayesian contained the people who had stood by Lynch through his 13-year-long legal ordeal. Beside him in the master suite was his wife of 22 years, Angela Bacares, a former vice president in the investment division of Deutsche Bank who had caught his eye while working an Autonomy deal. Other cabins housed the Clifford Chance attorneys who had orchestrated Lynch’s legal victory, as well as longtime colleagues, their partners, and a 1-year-old baby, all supported by 10 crew members. Also onboard was Lynch’s younger daughter, Hannah, 18, who was about to begin her studies at Oxford. Her older sister, Esme, 22, had remained in London.
paragraphs.zh: Bayesian 游艇上的舱室里，坐着在林奇长达 13 年法庭风波中始终站在他身边的人。与他在主套房同坐的是他结婚 22 年的妻子安吉拉·巴卡雷斯，这位德意志银行投资部门前副总裁曾在参与 Autonomy 一笔交易时吸引了林奇。其他舱位里住着 Clifford Chance 的律师，他们曾主导林奇的法律胜诉，也有多位长期同事、他们的伴侣，以及一名一岁的婴儿，船上另有 10 名船员在照看。林奇的次女汉娜（Hannah），18 岁，也在船上，即将前往牛津大学开始学业。她的姐姐艾丝梅（Esme）22 岁，仍留在伦敦。

paragraphs.en: The day before, their last of the trip, the group had been subdued. They had spent the afternoon in the laid-back coastal town of Cefalù, wandering the plaza and visiting the church. That night, they had gathered for dinner in the saloon of the Bayesian, followed by dessert and drinks on the upper deck. Everyone was in bed by 12:30 am. Water taxis were due early the next morning to take them to Palermo for their flights home.
paragraphs.zh: 在行程前一天，也就是最后一天，他们的情绪都很平静。当天下午，他们在风格悠闲的西西里海岸小镇凯富卢（Cefalù）消磨了时光，漫步广场并参观了教堂。那晚，他们在 Bayesian 的会客厅共进晚餐，之后到上层甲板上吃甜点、喝饮料。大家在凌晨 12:30 之前都已就寝。次日清晨，水上出租船将按时到来，载他们去往巴勒莫，随后赶往各自的回程航班。

paragraphs.en: Many of them would not make it. Griffiths’ video was to be the final earthly record of the Bayesian. Two minutes after he posted it, wind and rain started to buffet the vessel, and the young deckhand hurried to close forward hatches and cockpit windows. At 4 am, he ran down the stairs to wake the captain. This is the first time that details of Griffiths’ account, as well as those of many associates of Lynch, are being shared—WIRED interviewed dozens of friends and colleagues of Lynch, reviewed thousands of pages of court documents, and reconstructed events on the Bayesian based on accounts, yacht GPS data, and official investigative reports. The Bayesian and its human cargo were about to face forces that would test every calculation of the yacht’s design, the terrifying and tragic culmination of a series of highly improbable events. To this day, mystery surrounds the final night of Lynch’s life, making room for conspiracy theories about spies and secret hard drives that can’t seem to be quashed.
paragraphs.zh: 他们当中的许多人都不会再回到明天。格里菲斯的这段视频将成为 Bayesian 的最后一份世间记录。视频发布后两分钟，风雨就开始肆虐这艘船，年轻的甲板工作人员赶紧去关闭船首舱口和驾驶舱窗户。凌晨 4 点，他冲下楼梯去叫醒船长。此前，这是首次公开格里菲斯的叙述细节，也首次公开林奇多位同伴的说法。WIRED 采访了数十位林奇的朋友和同事，查阅了成千上万页法庭文件，并根据证词、游艇 GPS 记录与官方调查报告，重建了 Bayesian 上发生的事件。Bayesian 及其乘客即将面对一系列力量的考验，所有关于游艇设计的计算都将被推到极限，最终在一连串高度不可能发生的事件中走向恐怖而悲剧的结局。迄今为止，林奇生命最后一夜仍笼罩在谜团之中，这也给“间谍”“神秘硬盘”等阴谋论留下了空间，始终难以完全平息。

paragraphs.en: Today, Bayesian analysis is everywhere—powering spam filters, search engines, medical diagnoses, and artificial intelligence. Silicon Valley engineers casually talk about “updating their priors” as they refine machine-learning models. But in the 1980s, when Mike Lynch was a PhD student at Cambridge, Bayesian inference was still fighting for respectability, dismissed by many statisticians as unscientific because it dared to incorporate earlier beliefs into its calculations.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，贝叶斯分析无处不在——它为垃圾邮件过滤、搜索引擎、医疗诊断和人工智能提供支撑。硅谷工程师在优化机器学习模型时，常常随口说着“更新先验（updating their priors）”。但在20世纪80年代，当迈克·林奇还是剑桥大学的博士生时，贝叶斯推断还在努力争取学术尊重，许多统计学家认为它不够科学，因为它大胆把先前的信念也纳入计算。

paragraphs.en: Lynch had arrived at Cambridge in 1983 to study natural sciences, carrying with him the determination of someone who had started life in the red—minus £4, to be exact, the amount his Irish immigrant parents owed the bank after they got married. His father was a fireman from Cork, his mother a nurse from Tipperary. Growing up in 1970s London as an Irish boy meant learning to navigate prejudice during the height of the IRA bombings. “You had to learn to run fast,” he would later say, “but reading the room is a good thing to have as a skill.”
paragraphs.zh: 林奇于1983年来到剑桥学习自然科学，带着一种近乎从“负债起步”里养成的决心——准确来说，是负4英镑，这是他爱尔兰移民父母结婚后欠银行的金额。他的父亲是科克出身的消防员，母亲是蒂珀雷里来的护士。1970年代在伦敦长大、又是爱尔兰裔少年，意味着要在爱尔兰共和军爆炸活动最激烈的时候，学会在偏见中生存。他后来回忆道：“你必须学会跑得快，”但“读懂现场局势”同样是一项很重要的能力。

paragraphs.en: He had won a scholarship to Bancroft’s School—money bequeathed in the 1700s for the education of “poor boys”—and spent three hours a day commuting to Woodford Green, often hitchhiking and listening to drivers talk about their lives. On weekends, he practiced clarinet and saxophone and worked at his mother’s hospital, progressing from mopping floors to serving tea to patients. It was there, talking to the dying, that he learned a fundamental lesson: “Get on with it. Do stuff. Whatever it is you want to do, just do it.”
paragraphs.zh: 他获得了班克罗夫特学校的奖学金——这笔资金始于1700年代，专门用于“贫困男孩”的教育——并且每天通勤三个小时到伍兹福德格林，上下学时常常搭便车，还会听司机聊起他们的生活。周末，他练习单簧管和萨克斯风，并在母亲的医院打工，从拖地板做起，后来又给病人端茶水。也是在这里，在与临终者的对话中，他领悟到一句根本性的原则： “行动起来，做事。无论你想做什么，尽管去做。”

paragraphs.en: Lynch’s path to corporate software began at a distant remove—he wanted to build a digital music synthesizer. (It was the ’80s, after all.) Samplers like the Fairlight CMI cost around £18,000 to £30,000. So Lynch, who had been tinkering with electronics since childhood and tried starting bands, created a groundbreaking program for the Atari ST computer that could manipulate sound with “an accuracy of one sample—that’s one 50,000th of a second,” according to a 1988 interview with Sound on Sound magazine. The software gave musicians capabilities that were normally out of reach at the time. These technical obsessions led Lynch to Peter Rayner’s signal processing group at Cambridge, in the engineering department. Lynch had switched from natural sciences to electrical sciences in his third year. “I initially thought he was a bit lazy,” Rayner told me, recalling how Lynch often hadn’t done the assigned work. “But when we worked through problems in supervision, he showed great insight and came up with sometimes incorrect approaches, but often very interesting ones.” Rayner’s lab had a distinctly commercial bent, focused on solving real-world problems. Many of his roughly 100 PhD students went on to found companies, becoming, he said, full professors, multimillionaires, and even one billionaire—Lynch.
paragraphs.zh: 林奇进入企业软件领域的起点相当曲折——他最初想做一台数字音乐合成器。（毕竟那是80年代。）像 Fairlight CMI 这样的采样器当时要价约18,000到30,000英镑。于是林奇——他从小就爱折腾电子产品，还试着组过乐队——为当时的 Atari ST 电脑写出一款开创性的程序，能够以“单一样本精度”处理声音，换算下来是五万分之一秒，正如1988年他在《Sound on Sound》杂志的一次采访所说。这款软件让音乐人拥有当时通常无法触及的能力。这些技术痴迷把林奇带到了剑桥工程系的彼得·雷纳（Peter Rayner）信号处理小组。林奇在大三时从自然科学转向电子科学。雷纳对我说：“我起初还以为他有点懒，”他回忆到林奇经常没有按时完成被分配的作业。“但当我们在指导时一起攻克问题，他就会表现出很强的洞察力，提出的方法有时不完全正确，但往往非常有启发性。”雷纳的实验室明显更偏向商业化，专注于现实世界的问题解决。他提到，自己大约一百名博士生里，许多人后来都创办了公司，成为终身教授、千万富翁，甚至一位亿万富翁——就是林奇。

paragraphs.en: At Cambridge, Lynch discovered that the same mathematics he was using to clean up audio samples could be applied to any kind of noisy data. At the heart of this approach was Bayesian inference, a way of thinking about probability that had been developed by Thomas Bayes, a Presbyterian minister in the 1700s who died before publishing his findings. They were discovered by a friend who happened upon them when cleaning out Bayes’ things.
paragraphs.zh: 在剑桥，林奇意识到他用来清理音频样本的同一套数学方法，也可以用于任何类型的噪声数据。这种方法的核心是贝叶斯推断，一种关于概率的思考方式，由18世纪的一位长老会牧师托马斯·贝叶斯提出；可惜他在发表研究成果前去世。后来这些成果由一位朋友“偶然”在整理贝叶斯遗物时重新发现。

paragraphs.en: To the outside world, Mike Lynch appeared to be exactly what Europe needed: a homegrown technology champion who could compete with Silicon Valley.
paragraphs.zh: 在外界看来，Mike Lynch 似乎正是欧洲所需要的：一个能够与硅谷抗衡的本土科技旗手。

paragraphs.en: The power of Bayesian thinking lies in how it handles uncertainty. As Rayner explained to me, using a simple example: “If I gave you a coin and you tossed it and it came down heads, then all you could say based on that one experiment is the probability of tossing a head is 1—certain. But Bayes would say, ‘Come on, we know it’s going to be around a half.’ It allows you to take account of previous knowledge.”
paragraphs.zh: 贝叶斯思维的力量在于它处理不确定性的方式。Rayner 曾向我解释道，用一个简单例子：”如果我给你一枚硬币，你掷出后是正面，那么仅凭这一次实验你只能说掷出正面的概率是1——完全确定。可贝叶斯会说：‘别闹了，我们知道它大约是二分之一。’它让你能够把先前的知识考虑进去。“

paragraphs.en: This idea, of starting with prior assumptions and updating them with new evidence, seems simple, but Bayesian methods proved extremely effective at pattern recognition in the age of computers. Lynch’s doctoral thesis, completed in 1990, applied these techniques to neural networks for pattern classification—“a small step along the road” to modern AI, as Rayner put it. But Lynch saw commercial potential where others saw only academic interest. While supposedly working as a postdoc, he was often absent from the lab, secretly raising money for his first company. “Later on, I said to him, ‘Why on earth didn’t you come to me, Mike?’” Rayner recalled. “He said, ‘I didn’t want you to know!’”
paragraphs.zh: 这种从先验假设出发，再用新证据不断更新的想法看似简单，但贝叶斯方法在计算机时代的模式识别中被证明极为有效。Lynch 于1990年完成的博士论文将这些技术应用于神经网络的模式分类——Rayner 称其为通往现代人工智能道路上的“一小步”。但 Lynch 在其中看到了商业潜力，而其他人只看到学术价值。在他名义上担任博士后期间，他常常从实验室“消失”，暗地里为他的第一家公司筹款。Rayner 回忆道：“后来我对他说：‘你当初怎么不来找我呢，Mike？’‘他说：‘我不想让你知道！’”

paragraphs.en: That company was Cambridge Neurodynamics, founded after he completed his PhD, and its flagship product was a fingerprint recognition system that could handle smudged or partial prints. After Cambridge Neurodynamics, Lynch founded Autonomy. The new company would take the same ideas even further, applying them to the explosion of unstructured data that businesses were generating but couldn’t effectively search or analyze.
paragraphs.zh: 那家公司是 Cambridge Neurodynamics，成立于他完成博士后。它的主打产品是一套指纹识别系统，能够识别有污迹或缺失部分的指纹。随后，Lynch 又创立了 Autonomy。新公司将这些思路进一步推进，将其应用于企业正在产生但却难以有效检索和分析的海量非结构化数据。

paragraphs.en: The company’s breakthrough was IDOL—Intelligent Data Operating Layer—a pattern-recognition engine that could understand the actual meaning within human-generated information. Unlike keyword searches that might confuse different terminology, IDOL could recognize that a document about “automobiles” was relevant to a search for “cars.” It could identify concepts, find patterns, and extract meaning from chaos. The name Autonomy itself reflected Lynch’s vision: software intelligent enough to operate independently, to make its own guesses about meaning and relevance without human intervention. As Lynch would later explain in a 2000 interview with WIRED, “Bayes gave us a key to a secret garden. A lot of people have opened up the gate, looked at the first row of roses, said, ‘That’s very nice,’ and shut the gate. They don’t realize there’s a whole new country stretching out behind those roses.”
paragraphs.zh: 该公司突破性的成果是 IDOL（Intelligent Data Operating Layer，智能数据操作层），这是一种能理解人类生成信息中真实含义的模式识别引擎。与可能混淆不同术语的关键词搜索不同，IDOL 能够识别一份关于“汽车（automobiles）”的文档与“cars”的搜索词相关。它能够识别概念、发现模式，并从混乱的信息中提取意义。公司名称 Autonomy 本身就体现了林奇的愿景：软件足够智能，可以独立运行，能够在不需要人工干预的情况下自己判断意义与相关性。正如林奇后来在 2000 年接受《WIRED》采访时所解释的，“贝叶斯（Bayes）为我们打开了一把通往秘密花园的钥匙。很多人只是一推开大门，看到第一排玫瑰，就说‘真好看’，然后把门关上。他们没有意识到，在那些玫瑰后面还延伸着一整片崭新的大陆。”

paragraphs.en: Lynch had always been more interested in how people thought than what they knew. I talked to one of his employees, Emily Orton, who still remembers her first interview with Lynch back in 2009. While other executives had asked about her qualifications and experience, Lynch wanted to see how she dealt with the unexpected. He asked her a single question: “Tell me what makes you angry?”
paragraphs.zh: 林奇一向更关注人是如何思考的，而不是他们知道了什么。我采访了他的一位员工 Emily Orton，她仍然记得自己在 2009 年与林奇的第一次面试。其他高管当时问的是她的资历和经验，而林奇想知道的是她如何应对突发情况。他只问了一个问题：“告诉我，什么会让你生气？”

paragraphs.en: By that point, Autonomy was Britain’s largest software company. Its software was being used by intelligence agencies, law enforcement, and major corporations worldwide, and its acquisition, in 2005, of Verity—a search company double Autonomy’s size in terms of sales—eventually led to a market value north of $6 billion. Lynch earned the nickname “Britain’s Bill Gates.”
paragraphs.zh: 到那个时候，Autonomy 已经成为英国最大的纯软件公司。其软件已被全球的情报机构、执法部门和大型企业采用，而它在 2005 年并购 Verity——一家规模是其销售额两倍的搜索公司——最终把市值推高到 60 亿美元以上。林奇因此赢得了“英国版比尔·盖茨”的绰号。

paragraphs.en: Andy Kanter, an American lawyer at Autonomy since the late ’90s, had watched Lynch evolve from entrepreneur to CEO of a major corporation. He was a perfectionist, says Kanter. When Kanter and a colleague spent weeks crafting the IPO prospectus, Lynch read it, declared it “entirely wrong,” then approved it after they changed perhaps 10 words. “If something wasn’t right, he would dismiss it as useless,” Kanter said. “Ninety-five percent good isn’t good enough.”
paragraphs.zh: 安迪·坎特（Andy Kanter）是 Autonomy 从 1990 年代末就在职的一位美国律师，他见证了林奇从企业家成长为大型公司的首席执行官。他说，林奇是个完美主义者。坎特和一位同事花了几周时间起草 IPO 招股说明书，林奇读完后说“完全不对”，然后在他们修改了大约十个词后才批准通过。“如果有什么地方不对劲，他就会把它当作无用之物，”坎特说，“95% 的正确还不够好。”

paragraphs.en: But this harshness—one of his own lawyers told me that “he could be a prick”—came with fierce loyalty. For Christmas parties, Lynch flew the entire UK staff to four-star hotels in European cities—Venice, Prague. And quarter after quarter, the company continued to beat analyst expectations. To the outside world, Autonomy appeared to be exactly what Europe needed: a homegrown technology champion that could compete with Silicon Valley.
paragraphs.zh: 但这种严苛——他的一位律师对我说“他可能很难伺候”——伴随着近乎狂热的忠诚。在圣诞聚会上，林奇会把整个英国团队飞到欧洲各城的四星级酒店，比如威尼斯、布拉格。并且一季接一季，公司持续超出分析师预期。对外界而言，Autonomy 似乎正是欧洲所需：一个能够与硅谷一较高下的本土科技冠军。

paragraphs.en: But maintaining Autonomy’s image of consistent growth required increasingly creative measures. At the center of this effort was Sushovan Hussain, the CFO who had joined in 2001. Lynch and Hussain’s relationship went back to their school days—though Lynch would later downplay this, testifying that Hussain was merely a “third-level acquaintance” who had arrived at Bancroft’s in his final year. Yet they had both gone on to Cambridge and kept in touch over the years, and Lynch had attended Hussain’s wedding. When Hussain returned to England after working in the oil industry abroad, Lynch hired him.
paragraphs.zh: 但要维持 Autonomy 持续增长的形象，就需要越来越有“创意”的手段。此次努力的核心人物是 2001 年加入的首席财务官苏绍万·侯赛因（Sushovan Hussain）。林奇与侯赛因的关系可以追溯到他们的求学时期——尽管林奇后来淡化了这层关系，在证词中称侯赛因只是“第三层次的熟人”，是在他在 Bancroft 的最后一年才来的。然而，两人后来都去了剑桥，并多年来一直保持联系，林奇也出席了侯赛因的婚礼。当侯赛因在海外石油行业工作后返回英格兰时，林奇把他请了进来。

paragraphs.en: Software companies are judged differently than other businesses. Investors expect them to maintain high profit margins, because software costs almost nothing to reproduce—once you write the code, you can sell it infinitely without manufacturing costs. But, inevitably, some quarters fall short. So Lynch and Hussain developed practices designed to smooth out the randomness.
paragraphs.zh: 软件公司与其他行业的企业评估方式不同。投资者期望它们维持高利润率，因为软件几乎没有复制成本——一旦代码写好，就可以无限次销售而无需承担制造成本。但结果总会有波动，个别季度难免不达预期。于是林奇和侯赛因就制定了旨在平滑这种随机波动的做法。

paragraphs.en: The Autonomy acquisition, meant to herald HP’s transformation, instead became the symbol of a company in chaos.
paragraphs.zh: 原本应当标志 HP 转型的 Autonomy 收购，最终却成了混乱公司的一面旗帜。

paragraphs.en: The most straightforward was hardware reselling. When software sales fell short, Autonomy would purchase servers from manufacturers like EMC, Dell, and Hitachi, then resell them to customers—often at a loss. This wasn’t unusual; many software companies bundled hardware with their products. What was distinctive, according to later court findings, was how Autonomy accounted for these transactions. Instead of recording all the hardware costs as “cost of goods sold,” which would have devastated those crucial gross margins, portions were allocated to “sales and marketing expenses.” This preserved the illusion of a pure software company while pumping up revenue. The company also developed complex, opportunistic arrangements with resellers. During analyst calls, when hedge funds would try to trip up Hussain with coordinated questions about the numbers, Lynch would protect, and at times coach, his CFO.
paragraphs.zh: 最直接的是硬件转售。当软件销售不达标时，Autonomy 会从 EMC、戴尔和日立等厂商购买服务器，再转卖给客户——通常还要亏本。这并不罕见；许多软件公司都会把硬件与自家产品捆绑销售。与众不同的是，根据后来法院的调查认定，Autonomy 在记账上处理这些交易的方式。公司没有把全部硬件成本计入“销售成本”，那样会彻底摧毁其关键的毛利率，而是将其中一部分计入“销售与市场推广费用”。这既维持了其看似纯软件公司的形象，也抬高了营收。公司还与分销商建立了复杂而机会主义的合作安排。在分析师电话会议上，当对冲基金试图通过一连串协调提问来刺探数字漏洞、难住侯赛因时，林奇会出面护航，甚至在某些时候给财务长做当场指引。

paragraphs.en: So business stayed booming, and Lynch lived the life of a quirky multimillionaire. He had moved his family to a sprawling 69-acre farm he owned in Suffolk, complete with gardens, parkland, paddocks, and woodland. He restored a water mill and began breeding rare animals—“cows that became defunct in the 1940s and pigs that no one’s kept since medieval times,” as he would later describe them. He loved dogs, especially the rare Otter Hound. His dogs were all named after engineering parts: Switch, Tappet, Pinion, Valve, and Cam.
paragraphs.zh: 于是生意持续繁荣，林奇过上了怪癖百万富翁的生活。他把家人搬到了自己在萨福克拥有的 69 英亩广阔农庄，那里有花园、林地、放牧场和林荫地。他修复了一座水磨坊，并开始饲养稀有动物——“那些在 20 世纪40年代已绝迹的奶牛，以及自中世纪以来没人再养过的猪”，他后来这样描述。

paragraphs.en: Politicians sought out his advice for Britain’s technological future, and he was a popular speaker at events and in the business media. Everything Lynch touched, it seemed, turned to gold.
paragraphs.zh: 政客们会征求他对英国科技未来的建议，他也常受邀在各类活动和商业媒体上发言。似乎林奇碰到的每一件事都能变成“金子”。

paragraphs.en: Leo Apotheker had been CEO of Hewlett-Packard for less than a year when he announced his vision to transform the technology giant: It was time for HP to pivot from its roots in hardware manufacturing to become a software and services company. It was March 2011, and Apotheker needed a flagship acquisition to demonstrate this new direction. By July, Lynch and Apotheker were meeting in Deauville, the French seaside resort.
paragraphs.zh: 利奥·阿波塞克尔在担任惠普首席执行官还不到一年时，就宣布了其转型愿景：惠普应当从以往以硬件制造为根基的模式转向软件和服务型公司。这是在2011年3月，而阿波塞克尔需要一笔标志性收购来证明这一新方向。到7月，林奇与阿波塞克尔已在法国海滨度假胜地德维尔会面。

paragraphs.en: HP initially proposed acquiring Autonomy for between £24.94 and £26.94 per share in late July 2011. When market volatility drove down Autonomy’s stock price in early August, HP attempted to renegotiate lower, but Lynch held firm, refusing to accept anything below £25. Within weeks of that first proposal, the companies settled on £25.50 per share—approximately $11.1 billion total, a 64 percent premium over Autonomy’s market value. HP’s due diligence was, as Andy Kanter would later describe it, shocking in its brevity: “Having run billions and billions of dollars of acquisitions,” he told me, “I’d never seen anything like it.” The exact duration would become a matter of dispute—Lynch’s lawyers would claim it amounted to just six hours in conference calls, while HP described hundreds of people involved and consultations with Deloitte, Autonomy’s auditor.
paragraphs.zh: 2011 年 7 月下旬，惠普最初提议以每股 24.94 至 26.94 英镑收购 Autonomy。8 月初，市场波动压低了 Autonomy 的股价后，惠普试图重新谈判以更低价格收购，但林奇立场坚定，拒绝接受低于每股 25 英镑的报价。在最初提议后的几周内，双方敲定每股 25.50 英镑的价格——总计约 111 亿美元，相当于当时 Autonomy 市值的 64% 溢价。正如安迪·坎特后来形容的，惠普的尽职调查“短得惊人”。“我做过数十亿、上百亿美元规模的并购，”他对我说，“但从来没见过这种情况。”确切时长后来成为争议焦点——林奇的律师称仅有六个小时的电话会议，而惠普则说有数百人参与，并与 Autonomy 的审计机构德勤进行了咨询。

paragraphs.en: On August 18, 2011, HP put out what Kanter called “some of the five craziest corporate things all at the same time.” In a single press release, the company announced the Autonomy acquisition, revealed it had missed its numbers, lowered guidance, wrote off previous acquisitions, and disclosed plans to potentially split the company and exit the PC business. The stock price plummeted. The Autonomy acquisition, meant to herald HP’s transformation, instead became the symbol of a company in chaos.
paragraphs.zh: 2011 年 8 月 18 日，惠普发布了安迪·坎特称其为“同时发生的五件最疯狂企业事件”之一。公司在一份新闻稿中同时宣布了收购 Autonomy 的消息，披露了业绩未达预期，调低了业绩指引，核销了此前的收购交易，并披露了可能拆分公司、退出个人电脑业务的计划。股价骤然下跌。原本本应标志惠普转型的 Autonomy 收购，反而成了混乱公司状态的象征。

paragraphs.en: HP’s board, watching their share price crater, seemed to panic. About a month after the announcement—before the deal had even closed—they fired Apotheker and replaced him with Meg Whitman. The acquisition closed in October, but by then Autonomy had already become, in Lynch’s words, “the unwanted stepchild.” By May 2012, Whitman fired Lynch and reshuffled most of Autonomy’s senior leadership. Six months later, HP was writing down $8.8 billion of Autonomy’s value, with $5 billion attributed to what it called “serious accounting improprieties, disclosure failures and outright misrepresentations” at Autonomy.
paragraphs.zh: 惠普董事会看到股价崩跌后似乎陷入了恐慌。公告后约一个月——在交易尚未完成之前——他们解雇了阿波特克并由梅格·惠特曼接任。收购在 10 月完成，但到那时，Autonomy 已经在林奇的话里，变成了“讨厌的继子”。到 2012 年 5 月，惠特曼解雇了林奇，并调整了 Autonomy 大部分高层管理团队。六个月后，惠普减记了 Autonomy 88 亿美元的价值，其中 50 亿美元被归因于惠普所称的“严重会计不当、信息披露失败以及公然误导性陈述”。

paragraphs.en: From Lynch’s perspective, the narrative was clear: HP had thrown a Hail Mary, the market had punished the company for it, and now it needed someone to blame. HP saw it differently. In its telling, it had been systematically deceived, the victim of a sophisticated fraud that had inflated Autonomy’s value. The battle lines were drawn. HP sued Lynch in the UK for $5 billion. The US Department of Justice launched a criminal investigation. Lynch countersued, claiming HP had destroyed his reputation and mismanaged the acquisition. What had begun as a transformative deal had become one of the most bitter corporate disputes in recent history.
paragraphs.zh: 在林奇看来，故事很清楚：惠普抛出了一个‘大远射式赌注’，市场对这种行为施加了惩罚，而现在公司只是在找替罪羊。惠普则持不同观点。在他们的叙述中，自己是被系统性欺骗的受害者，是一场复杂骗局的牺牲品，这场骗局抬高了 Autonomy 的估值。双方的对立线因此拉开。惠普在英国起诉林奇，索赔 50 亿美元。美国司法部展开刑事调查。林奇反诉，称惠普毁掉了他的声誉并且并购执行不当。最初被视作“变革性交易”的事件，最终演变为近年最激烈的企业争端之一。

paragraphs.en: When HP announced its write-down in November 2012, Lynch had a choice. He could remain silent, let lawyers handle negotiations, perhaps reach a settlement. Instead, he went on Channel 4’s main business program and blamed HP for running a broken company. “In a year, they destroyed that value that was created over 10 years,” he said.
paragraphs.zh: 2012年11月惠普宣布减记时，林奇有选择。 他本可以保持沉默，让律师处理谈判，也许达成和解。可他却登上了英国第四频道的主打商业节目，指责惠普把公司经营成了一艘烂船。“一年之内，他们摧毁了十年间创造的价值，”他说。

paragraphs.en: That drove HP to double down even more. It made a criminal complaint in the US and filed a civil case in the UK. Against the advice of his legal team, Lynch insisted on fighting the UK civil case first. He had his team build new software to analyze the more than 11 million documents in the case. At times, he’d gather his legal team aboard the Bayesian—purchased just a year earlier—for strategy sessions.
paragraphs.zh: 这反而促使惠普进一步加码。惠普在美国提起了刑事诉讼，并在英国提起了民事诉讼。尽管律师团队反对，林奇坚持先打英国的民事案。为此，他让团队建立了新的软件，用来分析案件中超过1100万份文件。林奇时常在贝叶斯游艇（Bayesian）上召开战略会议——这艘游艇是在一年前刚买下的。

paragraphs.en: Lynch also continued to hobnob with British politicians and founded a new venture capital company, Invoke Capital, in 2012, assembling about 60 former Autonomy employees. Most of the money in the fund came from his personal fortune from the HP sale, about $800 million. Invoke’s portfolio spun out multiple highly successful companies, with traces of Bayesian inference in each one. Darktrace became a global force in AI-powered cybersecurity, valued at nearly $5 billion in its 2024 acquisition by Thoma Bravo. Featurespace’s fraud-detection technology became standard for major banks, with a proposed £700 million acquisition by Visa. Sophia Genetics went public in 2021 at a $1.14 billion valuation.
paragraphs.zh: 林奇也持续在英国政客圈子里活动，并于2012年创办了新的风险投资公司 Invoke Capital，汇聚了大约60名前 Autonomy 员工。基金中的大部分资金来自他在出售惠普股份时积累的个人财富，约为8亿美元。Invoke 的投资组合中涌现出多家极其成功的公司，每一家都带有一定的贝叶斯推断影子。Darktrace 在人工智能驱动的网络安全领域成为全球强者，在2024年被 Thoma Bravo 收购，估值接近50亿美元。Featurespace 的反欺诈技术成为主要银行的标准配置，先前曾拟被 Visa 以7亿英镑收购。Sophia Genetics 于2021年上市，估值达11.4亿美元。

paragraphs.en: Most white-collar defendants stay silent; Lynch insisted on taking the stand.
paragraphs.zh: 大多数白领被告都会保持沉默；而林奇则坚持要出庭作证。

paragraphs.en: In this way, his wealth grew, but the legal threats hovered above him, an axe yet to drop. In 2018, Hussain, who had fought his criminal case first in the US, was convicted of conspiracy, wire fraud, and securities fraud. It was a bad omen to have a jury decide criminal liability, a much higher bar than a civil ruling.
paragraphs.zh: 就在这样发展的过程中，他的财富不断增长，但法律威胁始终悬在他头顶，像一把随时可能落下的斧头。2018年，曾先在美国打过刑事案的侯赛因被判谋划串谋、证券交易欺诈和证券欺诈罪。由陪审团裁定刑事责任是一个不祥的征兆，因为这比民事裁决要高得多。

paragraphs.en: Lynch’s civil trial ran for 93 days, and what emerged was deeply troubling. The court found that when Autonomy’s US finance chief Brent Hogenson raised alarms in 2010 about suspect sales, Lynch’s response was not to investigate but to suppress. He forwarded Hogenson’s concerns with instructions to use “the usual encryption,” warned that “emails have been known to escape,” and ultimately plotted to fire Hogenson—indicating that Lynch knew the allegations were true.
paragraphs.zh: 林奇的民事审判持续了93天，显现出的情节令人深感不安。法庭发现，2010年当Autonomy的美国财务负责人布伦特·霍根森提出可疑销售问题时，林奇的反应不是调查，而是掩盖。他转交了霍根森的担忧并指示使用“常规加密”，还警告说“电子邮件常常会外泄”，并最终策划解雇霍根森——这表明林奇明知指控属实。

paragraphs.en: The court delivered a devastating judgment in January 2022. In a 1,700-page ruling, the judge found that Lynch had been “aware of improprieties in Autonomy’s accounting practices” and had been “dishonestly involved in manipulating the accounts.” The systematic accounting practices weren’t just aggressive. They were, the judge concluded, a deliberate scheme to mislead. American prosecutors, who had been waiting for the UK proceedings to conclude, now had the ammunition they needed. Extradition proceedings, already in motion, gained momentum.
paragraphs.zh: 2022年1月，法院作出了一份毁灭性的判决。在一份共1,700页的裁定书中，法官认定林奇“明知Autonomy在会计做法中存在不当行为”，并“在篡改账目中不诚实地参与其中”。这些系统性的会计操作不仅仅是激进操作。法官结论是，它们是一套有意的误导计划。一直在等待英国程序结束的美国检察官，终于拿到了他们需要的证据。早已启动的引渡程序因此进一步加速。

paragraphs.en: Lynch’s forced travel to the United States in May 2023 marked the beginning of an extraordinary ordeal. Federal prosecutors in San Francisco charged him in a 16-count indictment that included conspiracy to commit wire fraud, wire fraud, securities fraud, and conspiracy. If convicted on all counts, the 57-year-old faced up to 25 years in prison—effectively a life sentence.
paragraphs.zh: 2023年5月，林奇被迫前往美国，标志着一段非凡折磨的开端。旧金山的联邦检察官以一份16项控罪起诉他，罪名包括串谋实施电汇诈骗、电汇诈骗、证券欺诈和共谋。如果他在所有指控上被定罪，这位57岁的男子将面临最高25年监禁——实际上等同于终身监禁。

paragraphs.en: Despite US prosecutors promising the English court that Lynch wouldn’t be incarcerated pretrial, Judge Charles Breyer immediately sent him to jail upon arrival, his lead attorney Reid Weingarten recalled. “That was probably the lowest moment.” He ended up in jail for only one day, though, after posting a $100 million bond. The mathematics of his situation became Lynch’s obsession. “What are the odds?” he would constantly ask his friends and lawyers, especially Weingarten, who found it maddening. “It was the stupidest question ever,” he would later recall. “There’s just too many variables.” At the same time, he respected Lynch’s genuine curiosity—“there was nothing he didn’t know about or didn’t want to know about,” from astrophysics to politics, culture, music, even American baseball.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管美国检察官向英国法院承诺林奇不会在审前被关押，主审律师里德·温加特回忆说，查尔斯·布雷耶法官却在他抵达后立刻将其送进了监狱。“那大概是他最低潮的时刻。”不过他最终只在监狱里待了一天，后来交了1000万美元保释金就被释放。他的处境几乎成了林奇的执念。“几率有多大？”他总是反复问朋友和律师，尤其是温加特，后来者形容这问题让他抓狂。“这是他提出过最愚蠢的问题，”温加特后来回忆道。“因为变量实在太多了。”与此同时，他也尊重林奇那种真诚的好奇心——“他想知道的一样都不少”，从天体物理到政治、文化、音乐，甚至是美国职业棒球。

paragraphs.en: The trial began in March 2024, with Lynch joined by his former VP of finance Stephen Chamberlain as codefendant. From the start, it was clear that Lynch’s team had it easier. Hussain’s conviction had taught them the playbook of US prosecutors, and they’d had years to ready a new defense. Each night, Lynch and his legal team would work out who the prosecution was going to bring the next day. They also hired a “shadow jury”—a barman and a clerk paid to sit through all 11 weeks of proceedings and register independent impressions.
paragraphs.zh: 审判于2024年3月开庭，林奇与其前财务副总裁斯蒂芬·钱伯兰一起作为共同被告出席。从一开始就清晰可见，林奇的辩护团队占了很大便宜。哈桑的定罪让他们摸清了美国检察官的“套路”，并且他们有多年时间准备全新的辩护方案。每天晚上，林奇和律师团队都会梳理检方次日将要提交的内容。同时他们还雇了一支“影子陪审团”——一名酒吧调酒师和一名文员，付费听取整整11周的全部程序并记录独立感受。

paragraphs.en: Most white-collar defendants stay silent; Lynch insisted on taking the stand. He presented himself as a down-to-earth British entrepreneur who had been victimized by American corporate incompetence. He walked the jury through his working-class background, his academic achievements. When prosecutors pressed him on specific transactions, he deflected skillfully—these were matters for the finance team, he was focused on technology and strategy.
paragraphs.zh: 多数白领犯罪被告都会保持沉默；但林奇坚持要上证人席。于是他把自己塑造成一个朴实的英国企业家，声称自己是被美国企业管理无能所害。他向陪审团讲述了自己的工人阶级出身和学术成就。当检察官追问具体交易时，他很娴熟地回避回答——那些是财务团队负责的事务，他本人只专注于技术和战略。

paragraphs.en: One of the most effective moments came when Lynch described the experience with HP. “I watched them take this beautiful company and just wreck it,” he told the jury, emotion creeping in. “And then they had the audacity to blame me for their incompetence.”
paragraphs.zh: 最有力的时刻之一出现在他谈及惠普时。“我亲眼看到他们把这家优秀的公司彻底毁掉，”他向陪审团说道，语气中有了些许情绪。“然后他们居然厚颜无耻地把自己的无能归咎于我。”

paragraphs.en: The verdict came on June 6, 2024. As the jury foreman read “not guilty” to all remaining charges, Lynch cried. So did his wife. Chamberlain was also acquitted on all counts. Speaking to journalists later, Lynch reflected on what he’d endured: “It’s bizarre, but now you have a second life,” he said. “The question is, what do you want to do with it?”
paragraphs.zh: 裁决在2024年6月6日作出。当陪审团书记宣读对所有剩余指控均为“无罪”时，林奇哭了出来。他的妻子也一样。查默伦同样在全部指控上被判无罪。林奇后来对记者说起自己所经历的一切时表示：“这太离奇了，但如今你有了第二次人生。”他补充道：“问题是，你想拿这第二次人生做什么？”

paragraphs.en: As part of his recovery process, Lynch planned a long summer aboard the Bayesian, full of friends and celebration. For one particular outing in August, he invited along everyone who stayed close to him during the darkest period of his life. Christopher Morvillo, the Clifford Chance partner who had helped quarterback the US legal strategy, was there with his wife, Neda. Jonathan Bloomer, the Morgan Stanley international executive who had served as a character witness, had accepted the invitation along with his wife, Judy.
paragraphs.zh: 在他的恢复过程中，林奇计划在“贝叶斯”号上度过一个漫长的夏季，满载朋友，尽情庆祝。8月的一次特别航行中，他邀请了那些在他人生最黑暗阶段始终陪伴在身边的人。与他一起前往的还有克利福德·张律师事务所的合伙人克里斯托弗·莫维洛，这位律师曾协助统筹其美国法律战略；他也带上了妻子内达。摩根士丹利国际业务高管、曾出庭作证的性格证人乔纳森·布鲁默同样接受了邀请，并与妻子朱迪一起前来。

paragraphs.en: The yacht itself was a 56-meter sailing vessel with a dark blue hull and a minimalist Japanese-style interior, later referred to by The Times of London as a “masterpiece of engineering and opulence.” The yacht’s original name was Salute; Lynch rechristened it the Bayesian. The vessel was magnificent but also an anomaly: It had a single, towering aluminum mast.
paragraphs.zh: 这艘游艇是一艘56米长的帆船，船体为深蓝色，内部采用简约的日式设计，后来被《伦敦时报》称为“工程与奢华的杰作”。这艘游艇最初叫“Salute”；林奇将其更名为“Bayesian”。这艘船虽然宏伟，却也很特别：它只有一根高耸的铝制桅杆。

paragraphs.en: When that hot air collided with colder masses higher up, it produced a rotating updraft—a supercell.
paragraphs.zh: 当这股热空气与更高层的冷空气团相遇时，就会形成一个旋转上升气流，即超级单体风暴（supercell）。

paragraphs.en: The following account is drawn from official investigation reports, videos, photos, and people familiar with the accounts of the crew and survivors. The August sailing was planned as a leisurely tour of Sicily’s northern coast and Aeolian Islands. The group started in Milazzo, then spent four days exploring the volcanic archipelago. They anchored off Isola di Vulcano one day for a few hours to watch the active crater glow against the sky, visited Panarea, and enjoyed the crystal clear waters around Dattilo. It was exactly the kind of relaxed, intimate celebration Lynch had envisioned. It was also a sendoff for Hannah, an aspiring poet. The two loved to spar over meals, arguing about politics and world events, with Lynch playing the contrarian.
paragraphs.zh: 以下内容汇集自官方调查报告、视频与照片，以及了解船员和幸存者陈述的人士。8月的这次航行本计划是一次轻松的行程，路线包括西西里北岸和风景如画的埃奥利亚群岛。船上人员先从米拉佐出发，随后在这组火山群岛停留了四天。他们有一天在维尔卡诺岛外抛锚了几个小时，观赏活火山口与夜空相映；也去了帕纳雷亚，并在达蒂洛周边享受了澄澈见底的海水。这正是林奇设想的那种轻松而私密的庆祝方式。这也是对汉娜的一次送别——她是一位有抱负的诗人。他们俩在餐桌上总爱展开辩论，围绕政治与时事争论不休，而林奇常常故意持反方观点。

paragraphs.en: That weekend, Lynch received two devastating calls from Andy Kanter about Stephen Chamberlain, his Autonomy codefendant. The first call, on Saturday, Lynch answered with a happy hello—laughter and cheer audible in the background—before Kanter delivered what he called “the gravest news”: Chamberlain, a middle-aged soccer fan and avid runner, had been struck by a car while jogging and suffered a traumatic head injury. By Sunday’s call, the news was worse: The hospital was turning off life support. The group aboard the Bayesian lit a candle for Chamberlain in the church at Cefalù.
paragraphs.zh: 那一周末，林奇接到了安迪·坎特打来的两通沉重来电，内容都与斯蒂芬·钱伯莱恩有关，他是林奇的“Autonomy”共同被告。第一次来电在星期六，林奇先是笑着热情接起电话，背景里还听得见笑声和愉快的谈话声；随后坎特递出了他所说的“最严重消息”：钱伯莱恩，这位中年足球迷、狂热跑者，晨跑时被一辆汽车撞倒，头部受到严重创伤。到星期天的那通电话，消息更糟：医院准备停止其生命维持治疗。贝叶森号船上全体人在切法鲁的教堂里为钱伯莱恩点上了蜡烛。

paragraphs.en: Weather forecasts for that night had mentioned possible thunderstorms, but nothing unusual for August in the Mediterranean. The five-day trip had been blessed with perfect conditions—clear skies, warm temperatures, and gentle winds. Lynch and his wife told the captain to move the Bayesian farther west to the village of Porticello to make the commute to the airport the following morning easier, in the event of rain.
paragraphs.zh: 当晚的天气预报提到了可能出现的雷阵雨，但在地中海的八月，这并不罕见。五天的行程一直以来都被祝福般的好天气所包围——晴朗的天空、温暖的气温和微和的风。林奇与妻子告诉船长把贝叶森号向西移到波尔蒂切洛港村停泊，以便次日一早前往机场时更方便，万一下雨的话也不至于太麻烦。

paragraphs.en: The crew settled into their nightly routines. The Bayesian sat at anchor around 300 meters from shore, her distinctive silhouette dark against the night sky.
paragraphs.zh: 船员照常开始了夜间的日常安排。贝叶森号在距岸约三百米处抛锚，船身独特的轮廓在夜空下显得黯淡。

paragraphs.en: Even as people turned in for the night, the flashes of lightning had been visible for some time—flaring from the west, far offshore where a dense cloud tower brooded—but nothing hinted it would reach the anchorage. Thunderstorm alerts were in force for Sicily’s north coast, yet forecasters expected only scattered cells. In Griffiths’ Instagram video, lightning flickers in the clouds, the flashes outlining their depths.
paragraphs.zh: 当人们陆续就寝时，远处西侧外海一大片浓云中一直有闪电出现——一道道电光在云层上闪烁——但当时没有任何迹象表明风暴会逼近停泊点。虽然天气预报已经对西西里北岸发布了雷暴警报，但气象部门预期只是零星的雷雨单体。在 Griffiths 的 Instagram 视频里，闪电在云层中闪烁跳动，划出了云层的层次深度。

paragraphs.en: Meteorological analysis later showed a perfect, if unlikely, recipe for disaster: Tyrrhenian surface water near 29 degrees Celsius—2 to 3 degrees above the norm—pumped moisture aloft like fuel into a furnace. When that hot air collided with colder masses higher up, it produced a rotating updraft—a supercell.
paragraphs.zh: 后来气象分析显示，这是一次几乎完美却又不太可能发生的灾难配方：提雷尼亚海表层水温接近29摄氏度，比常年高出2到3度，像燃料一样把湿气输送到高空，如同往炉膛里加柴。当这股热气团与更高空的冷空气团相遇时，便形成了旋转的上升气流——一条超级单体雷暴。

paragraphs.en: As Griffiths ran to shut the forward hatches, the wind was already past 30 knots; 5 miles away, it was recorded at 41 knots. By 3:57 am, the Bayesian began to drift under pressure from the wind, dragging its anchor. The yacht’s chief engineer, Timothy Parker Eaton, who had sleeping quarters near the engine, felt the movement. He made sure all three generators were operating and headed to the wheelhouse—the yacht’s enclosed main control room.
paragraphs.zh: 当 Griffiths 跑去关闭前舱口时，风已经超过30节；在距离其5英里的地方，风速被记录为41节。到凌晨3:57，Bayesian（贝叶斯号）就在风压下开始漂移，拖动了船锚。游艇首席工程师提摩西·帕克·伊顿（Timothy Parker Eaton）在发动机附近有睡眠区，他感受到了这种位移。他确认三台发电机都在运转，并前往舵楼——游艇的封闭式主控制室。

paragraphs.en: The vessel was lurching, but boats, of course, are always moving. Only some passengers woke up in the dark night. Key crew members—chief officer Tijs Koopmans and chief steward Sasha Murray among them—dressed hurriedly and made their way out of the crew’s quarters. Bacares pulled on her dressing gown and went to the saloon to check what was going on. Charlotte Golunski and her husband brought their baby there, too, to avoid waking the other guests. On deck, the crew worked to secure the vessel against the building storm, closing windows and securing cabinets. Above, on the fly deck, the chief steward met a wall of rain that she’d later describe as unlike anything she’d ever seen.
paragraphs.zh: 船体在晃动，但船只终究是会晃的。那天夜里只有少数乘客在黑暗中醒来。几名关键船员——包括大副蒂伊斯·科普曼（Tijs Koopmans）和首席男管家萨莎·默里（Sasha Murray）——匆忙穿好衣服，从船员舱快速走出来。巴卡雷斯（Bacares）披上晨衣，走到客厅查看情况。夏洛特·戈伦斯基（Charlotte Golunski）和她的丈夫也把宝宝带到了那里，以免惊醒其他客人。甲板上，船员们忙着将船固定在日益增强的风暴中，关闭窗户、固定柜门。甲板上层的飞行甲板上，首席男管家遇到了一片雨幕，她后来形容那场雨“与她以往见过的任何情况都不一样”。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, the chief officer climbed to the flying bridge, where Captain James Cutfield, just roused, ordered him to wake the rest of the crew. Below, a crew member passed the galley: Chef Recaldo Thomas was stowing pans. “Good morning!” he called, as if greeting just another squall.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，值班军官爬上了飞桥，刚刚被唤醒的船长 James Cutfield 命令他去叫醒其他船员。楼下，有一名船员经过厨房时，主厨 Recaldo Thomas 正在收拾锅具。 “早上好！”他喊道，仿佛只是在向又一次突来的阵雨打招呼。

paragraphs.en: By now the yacht was sliding south-southeast at 1.8 knots. The yacht’s 72-meter aluminum mast—taller than a 20-story building—caught the wind like a lever, and the boat started to heel. The chief officer confirmed with the chief engineer that engines and pumps were ready to reposition the boat.
paragraphs.zh: 此时游艇已以每小时1.8节向东南偏南滑行。游艇高达72米的铝制桅杆——比20层楼还高——像杠杆一样顶风，船体开始倾斜。值班军官向首席机械师确认，发动机和泵已准备好，以便将船只重新调整方向。

paragraphs.en: At 4:04 am the onboard CCTV caught the flying-bridge awning shredding in an instant, obliterated by the force of the gale. Just moments later, as Captain Cutfield grabbed the helm to swing the bow into the wind, the downburst struck. A shaft of cold air dropped from 30,000 feet and hit the sea like an invisible fist. Wind leapt from 30 knots to more than 70 knots, a hurricane-force gust that struck the Bayesian with devastating power. The weather service’s review of satellite data would later suggest bursts over 87 knots, or nearly 100 miles per hour. The yacht began to tip hard to starboard. Within 15 seconds it capsized, generators cutting out as the side hit the water. It was 4:06.
paragraphs.zh: 凌晨4点04分，船上的监控摄像头捕捉到飞桥遮阳篷在一瞬间被撕碎，瞬间被狂风风力摧毁。就在不久之后，当 James Cutfield 抓住舵轮试图将船头转向迎风时，突发下击暴流降临。一道自30000英尺俯冲下来的冷空气柱击中海面，仿佛一记无形的重拳。风速从30节骤增到70多节，达到飓风级阵风，以毁灭性的力量冲击着“Bayesian”。气象部门后来通过卫星数据复核，显示阵风一度超过87节，接近每小时100英里。游艇急剧向右舷倾覆。15秒内，它倾覆，发电机在船侧触水的一刻停转。那是4点06分。

paragraphs.en: People, furniture, and glass hurled sideways as battery lights flickered on, and then off. Bacares, the Golunski family, and a steward slammed into a wall—now the floor—and were cut by shattering glass. Griffiths, thrown from the flying bridge into the sea, clawed back aboard. Records indicate that the alarm was never pulled; no one had time.
paragraphs.zh: 人、家具和玻璃都被抛向一侧，电池灯闪烁后又熄灭。Bacares、Golunski 家族以及一名服务员撞向了墙壁——那时那面墙已变成地面——并被碎玻璃划伤。Griffiths 从飞桥被掀入海中后爬回船上。记录显示警报从未被拉响；因为那一刻，任何人都来不及去做。

paragraphs.en: Below, guests Matthew Fletcher and Ayla Ronald escaped their forward cabin by pulling out drawers as steps. Their cabin was farthest from the stairwell—the stairwell being a pivotal location, a straight shot to the saloon, which was the best way to exit the ship. Water poured over the rails. The yacht was flooding fast.
paragraphs.zh: 在下面，客人马修·弗莱彻和艾拉·罗纳德从前舱逃生时，把抽屉当作台阶往上爬。他们的舱室离楼梯间最远——而楼梯间是关键位置，直通沙龙（主厅）的通道，也是离船逃生的最佳路线。船舷的护栏上已被海水淹没。游艇正在迅速进水。

paragraphs.en: In the saloon, a desperate human chain formed to pass people up toward the open air. The chief officer shoved Bacares up toward the central stairway, where water was now cascading down in a torrent, and another crew member pulled her up onto the side of the flybridge above. Together they handed up Charlotte Golunski and the baby, passing them person-to-person through the flooding, tilted interior. But the situation was fast evolving. Three of them were momentarily trapped in an air pocket behind the wheelhouse door—James Emslie tried to force it open from outside but couldn’t, due to the pressure of the rushing water. Eventually, one of the crew members got it open when the boat had filled with enough water for the pressure to equalize.
paragraphs.zh: 在沙龙内，乘客们组成了一条急切的人链，互相托举向露天区域向上移动。首席副官把巴卡雷斯推向中央楼梯处，而那里水流正如洪流般倾泻下来，另一名船员把她从另一侧拉上了飞桥上方的平台。接着他们又把夏洛特·戈伦斯基和婴儿一起接力递上去，借助这条扭曲倾斜的船内通道，一步一步传递过去。局势变化非常快。三人一度被困在驾驶室门后方的一处空气袋里——船外侧来的詹姆斯·姆斯利试图从外部撬开门，但做不到，因为奔涌而入的水压太大。最终，随着船内蓄满足够水量、压力逐渐平衡，其中一名船员才把门打开。

paragraphs.en: The chief officer, lowest in the chain, was suddenly swept back into the main saloon before he could climb after the others. The ship, at this point, was slanted on an angle and the officer was in a corner on the upper part of the slant. He surfaced in a small air bubble there: The ceiling was on his side, the wall above him. His first escape attempt failed in the black water, which was clogged with cushions and broken bits of the once-lovely interior. Finding another small air pocket, he was able on the second attempt to feel his way along the debris-filled space to the heavy glass doors at the back of the saloon, braced his feet against the tilted door frame and hauled them open wide enough to squeeze through into the open sea. He later told Italian investigators he was sure he was going to die.
paragraphs.zh: 这条人链中最末端的首席副官在其他人还没来得及爬上前时，突然被湍流卷回主沙龙。此时船体已经倾斜，副官正站在倾斜面上的上方角落。此刻他在一个小小的空气袋里露出头来：天花板压在他的一侧，身上方是一堵墙。他第一次尝试逃离时，在被海绵般漆黑的水中失败了，水里塞满了垫子和曾经漂亮内饰碎裂下来的零件。找到另一个小空气袋后，他第二次尝试成功了：沿着满是残骸的空间摸索，摸到沙龙后方的厚重玻璃门，用脚抵在倾斜的门框上，拽开门足够大以便挤过，冲向开阔海面。他后来向意大利调查人员表示，他当时确信自己会死。

paragraphs.en: The remaining passengers and crew clung desperately to whatever they could find. At this point it was clear that there would be no saving the boat, only surviving its sinking. The storm, though, was over. The wind, on the surface of the water, was quiet. Outside in the water, Captain Cutfield tried to organize the abandonment of the superyacht, telling survivors to swim clear of the mast and the boom, which could drag someone down with it. Murray counted heads and Griffiths improvised a tourniquet on Emslie’s arm. The baby balanced on a cushion, absurdly, precariously. Then the group finally caught a break: Koopmans, the chief officer, surfaced and cut one life raft free right as other crew members were trying, and failing, to get to another one. He ferried it to the bobbing group and inflated it.
paragraphs.zh: 其余乘客和船员们抓住一切能抓住的东西，拼命求生。到这一步，已经清楚这艘船已经没法挽救，唯一能做的就是在其下沉中活下来。风暴已经过去。水面上的风很平静。就在水里，卡普特菲尔德船长尝试组织疏散这艘超级游艇，告诉幸存者要远离桅杆和横杆，因为它们会拖拽人入水而导致沉没。默里清点人数，格里菲斯替姆斯利的手臂临时扎了止血带。婴儿被放在一个垫子上，姿态摇摇欲坠，却又滑稽地平衡着。随后这群人终于迎来了转机：库普曼斯——这位首席副官——从水下冒出头来，刚好在其他船员正在试图却未能到达另一艘救生筏之际，解下了一艘救生筏。他把它送到一群正在上下起伏的幸存者身边并将其充气。

paragraphs.en: From the raft, the group watched the Bayesian’s bow rise up and then plunge down. By 4:24, the boat was gone, drifting 50 meters down to the seabed. From the time the full storm struck the yacht, only 18 minutes had passed.
paragraphs.zh: 从救生筏上，众人看着贝叶茜号的船头先抬起又猛然落下。到4:24，船已经消失了，沉到了海底下约50米处。从整场风暴真正撞击这艘游艇开始，到那一刻只过去了18分钟。

paragraphs.en: Ten minutes later, the chief engineer fired a red parachute flare, but higher-altitude wind carried it sideways. He fired a second, and a crew member aboard the 42-meter steel-hulled schooner Sir Robert Baden Powell, anchored nearby, spotted it and alerted the skipper. The downburst had struck like a scalpel; the other vessel, barely 100 meters away, lost nothing more than an awning frame.
paragraphs.zh: 十分钟后，首席工程师发射了一枚红色降落伞信号弹，但高空风把它吹向了一边。他又发射了第二枚，附近抛锚的42米钢底双桅纵帆船“罗伯特·巴登·鲍威尔亲王号”上的一名船员看见了，并立即向船长报告。下击暴流像手术刀般狠狠掠过；另一艘船与其相距不到100米，仅仅损坏了一根遮阳棚框架。

paragraphs.en: Its tender reached the raft and recovered 15 survivors. Bacares was among them, injured but alive. Her husband, daughter, and five others—Jonathan Bloomer, Judy Bloomer, Christopher Morvillo, Neda Morvillo, and Recaldo Thomas—remained inside the sunken yacht. Thomas—the chef who had called “Good morning!”—was found dead later that day.
paragraphs.zh: 它的舢板抵达救生筏，捞回了15名幸存者。巴卡雷斯也在其中，受了伤但还活着。她的丈夫、女儿以及另外五人——乔纳森·布鲁默（Jonathan Bloomer）、朱迪·布鲁默（Judy Bloomer）、克里斯托弗·莫维洛（Christopher Morvillo）、内达·莫维洛（Neda Morvillo）和雷卡尔多·托马斯（Recaldo Thomas）——仍留在已经沉没的游艇内。托马斯——那个曾喊过“早上好！”的厨师——当天稍后被发现已经死亡。

paragraphs.en: Lynch, as a baby. He grew up in a modest household, the son of Irish parents.
paragraphs.zh: 在Lynch还是婴儿的时候，他成长在一个普通家庭，是一对爱尔兰父母的儿子。

paragraphs.en: Lynch and his daughter Hannah, who was an aspiring poet.
paragraphs.zh: 林奇和他的女儿汉娜同样在场，她是一位有志于成为诗人的年轻人。

paragraphs.en: Rescue boats eventually arrived, too late to do anything about those stuck inside the sunken boat. By 6 am, as the survivors reached the dock at Porticello, villagers began throwing clothing out of their windows for the barely clad group.
paragraphs.zh: 随后救援船只终于赶到，但时机已晚，无法及时营救那些困在沉没船里的人员。早上六点，幸存者抵达波尔蒂切洛码头时，村民们已经开始从窗户往外扔衣物给这群几乎衣不蔽体的人。

paragraphs.en: Andy Kanter, who was due to join Lynch with another set of lawyers on the Bayesian the following week, flew to Sicily immediately upon hearing the news. As Lynch’s close friend and business associate, he found himself coordinating with authorities and helping Bacares navigate the bureaucratic maze that follows disasters. The man who had called Lynch just hours before the tragedy to tell him about Chamberlain’s life support now faced the impossible task of managing the aftermath of both men’s deaths. In an unforgettable coincidence, Chamberlain passed the following day—meaning both men had died from freak accidents, thousands of miles apart, roughly within 24 hours of each other. This would fuel wild conspiracy theories online for months.
paragraphs.zh: 安迪·坎特原本计划下周与另一批律师一同登船与林奇会面。听到消息后，他立即飞往西西里。作为林奇的密友和生意伙伴，他便开始与当局协同，并帮助巴卡雷斯应对灾难后那场通常漫长又混乱的官僚流程。这个人在悲剧发生前几小时刚给林奇打了电话，告诉他钱伯兰正在接受维生系统治疗；如今，他却不得不处理两人死亡后的善后工作。更诡异的是，钱伯兰是在次日去世的——也就是说，两人都是在距离万里之外、约在二十四小时内，分别死于离奇事故。这一细节在网上引发了长达数月的阴谋论狂潮。

paragraphs.en: The scene that greeted Kanter was surreal in its intensity. The media had descended on the small Sicilian port with overwhelming force. The hotel where survivors were staying shut down reservations for five days to accommodate the press invasion, says Kanter. Reporters were climbing into ambulances, desperate for any scrap of information. Italian media speculated wildly about encrypted hard drives in the yacht’s safe, with tabloids suggesting connections to Western intelligence services. The lack of verifiable information didn’t stop the stories: One newspaper made up an interview with Charlotte Golunski, where she’d said she momentarily lost her baby for “two seconds.” In truth, she hadn’t spoken to any journalist. In one particularly bizarre moment, while survivors were ensconced at a resort hotel, a 12-foot-tall minion walked by—a jarring reminder that normal life continued even as families dealt with unimaginable loss.
paragraphs.zh: 坎特抵达后看到的景象，几乎像梦境般离奇。媒体以压倒性的规模涌入这个小小的西西里港口。坎特说，幸存者下榻的酒店因此停业取消了五天的全部预订，以应对媒体席卷。记者们爬进救护车，急切地想得到任何一丁点信息。意大利媒体对游艇保险箱里的加密硬盘展开了各种猜测，八卦小报甚至暗示其与西方情报机构有关。缺乏可核实信息并未遏制谣言：有家报纸编造了夏洛特·戈伦斯基的一段“采访”，称她“短暂失去了自己的孩子两秒钟”。实际上，她并未与任何记者交谈。一次尤为怪诞的场景中，当幸存者住在度假村酒店时，一名身高十二英尺的伴舞人员走过走廊，这一幕刺痛人心地提醒着大家：在家庭成员承受难以想象的丧亲痛苦时，外面的日常生活却仍在继续。

paragraphs.en: What has emerged through the various investigations, and through press accounts, is a critical flaw in the Bayesian’s design.
paragraphs.zh: 在各种调查以及媒体报道中浮现出来的是，The Bayesian 的设计存在一个关键缺陷。

paragraphs.en: Even before the bodies were recovered, the blame game began. The yacht’s manufacturer initially filed a €222 million claim against Lynch’s wife and crew members, though it was quietly withdrawn. Insurance companies began their own investigations, with potentially $150 million in claims at stake. Quickly, Italian prosecutors opened an inquiry, initially focusing on potential charges of negligent shipwreck and manslaughter. Captain Cutfield faced particular scrutiny—questions about the keel position, open hatches, and whether adequate warnings were given. The prosecutors moved swiftly, interviewing everybody during those first five days. Since the boat’s party were all witnesses, not subjects, they were required to sit for their interviews, even though they were literally still shaking from their trauma.
paragraphs.zh: 在遗体被打捞出来之前，推诿责任的游戏就已经开始了。该游艇制造商最初向林奇的妻子和船员提起了 2.22 亿欧元的索赔诉讼，后来又悄然撤回。保险公司也随即展开了各自的调查，涉案索赔金额可能高达 1.5 亿美元。随后，意大利检察官很快启动了调查，最初重点关注是否涉嫌过失触礁和过失杀人。科特菲尔德船长受到特别关注，人们质疑船底龙骨位置、甲板舱口是否未关闭，以及是否发出了足够的警示。检方行动迅速，在最初五天里采访了每一个相关人员。由于船上当事人都是这次聚会的一员，全部是“在场者”而非“被疑者”，他们都被要求接受询问，即使那些人在创伤后身体仍在发抖。

paragraphs.en: The recovery operation took days. Divers worked in dangerous conditions, navigating the yacht’s flooded interior to reach the victims. Mike Lynch, the Bloomers, and the Morvillos—Christopher and Neda—were found clustered together in one cabin on the port side, suggesting they had sought shelter together as the yacht went down. Hannah was discovered in another cabin on the same side, by herself.
paragraphs.zh: 打捞行动持续了数天。潜水员在危险条件下作业，穿越这艘游艇内被洪水淹没的区域，才能到达遇难者所在处。迈克·林奇、Bloomers 夫妇以及 Morvillo 夫妇——克里斯托弗和内达——被发现时聚在左舷的同一间船舱内，似乎他们在船只下沉时一起寻求了避难处。汉娜则在同一侧的另一间船舱里单独被找到。

paragraphs.en: The following Sunday, townspeople organized a memorial service that culminated in a candlelight vigil where people released wreaths into the water. Still, the boat sat at the bottom of the sea. In May 2025, a Dutch diver working for a company trying to recover the yacht died, adding another life to the toll. Finally, in June, the yacht and mast were recovered and brought to a terminal nearby for further investigation. By this point, the crew had dispersed across the world and have mostly stayed quiet about the event. Griffiths, for his part, still plans on working in the yachting industry.
paragraphs.zh: 在随后一个星期日，镇民举办了纪念仪式，活动最终以烛光守夜结束，参与者向海里投放了花圈。尽管如此，船只仍静静躺在海底。2025 年 5 月，一名为打捞该游艇作业的荷兰潜水员在行动中遇难，又给死亡人数增加了一个数字。直到 6 月，该游艇及其桅杆才被打捞出水，并被运至附近码头进行进一步调查。此时，船员成员已经散落世界各地，并且大多对这起事件保持沉默。至于 Griffiths，他仍然计划继续在游艇行业工作。

paragraphs.en: In July, UK High Court judge Robert Hildyard ruled that Lynch’s estate and former Autonomy CFO Sushovan Hussain owed Hewlett-Packard more than £700 million ($940 million) in damages from the Autonomy acquisition. In a postscript to his judgment, Hildyard expressed his “sorrow at this devastating turn of events, and my sympathy and deepest condolences, having come to know and admire Dr Lynch (notwithstanding my findings against him) over the course of a very long trial.” The ruling, which represented a significant reduction from HP’s original $4 billion claim, nonetheless reportedly threatened to bankrupt the estate, estimated to be worth around £500 million. Hussain, now out of prison, has started a company to help former criminals find work.
paragraphs.zh: 今年7月，英国高等法院法官罗伯特·希尔德亚德裁定，林奇的遗产和前安托诺米公司首席财务官苏绍文·侯赛因（Sushovan Hussain）应向惠普（HP）支付超过7亿英镑（9.4亿美元）的损害赔偿金。希尔德亚德在判决附言中写道：“对这突如其来的灾难性转折，我深感悲痛；我对林奇（尽管裁决中认定了对他的不利结论）在漫长审理过程中了解到并钦佩他的人，表示我的同情和深切哀悼。” 这项裁决使 HP 最初4十亿美元的索赔要求大幅降了下来，但据报道仍足以威胁林奇遗产的生存，该遗产估值约为5亿英镑。侯赛因现已出狱，已创办了一家帮助前罪犯就业的公司。

paragraphs.en: The final reports on the Bayesian sinking have yet to be released. The UK’s Marine Accident Investigation Branch published an interim report in May 2025 that focused on design and environmental vulnerabilities—though Italian prosecutors are separately investigating the captain and two crew members for manslaughter and negligence. What has emerged through the various investigations, and through press accounts, is a critical flaw in the Bayesian’s design. The single-mast configuration that gave the yacht her elegant lines—unique among the boat designer’s sought-after series of similar vessels—also dangerously affected her center of gravity. The MAIB analysis, conducted by the Wolfson Unit at Southampton, found that with the keel raised and no sails set, the yacht became vulnerable to capsizing in winds exceeding 60 knots—a threshold crossed in seconds when the downburst struck. The other yachts in the same series as the Bayesian had two masts, not one. This problem might have been noticed earlier except for one other factor: Elite luxury yachts don’t have to comply with the same robust safety rules as commercial ones. The International Safety Management Code is voluntary for boats registered for private use.
paragraphs.zh: 关于“贝叶斯”号沉没的最终调查报告尚未发布。英国海事事故调查局（Marine Accident Investigation Branch）于2025年5月发布了一个中期报告，重点是设计缺陷和环境脆弱性——与此同时，意大利检方正在对船长及两名船员就过失致死和过失行为展开独立调查。通过各种调查以及媒体报道可以看到，“贝叶斯”号设计上的一个关键缺陷逐渐浮出水面。它那单桅配置赋予了游艇优雅的线条——在该船型设计师受追捧的同系列船中也属独特——却也危险地改变了重心。由南安普敦沃尔森（Wolfson）小组进行的 MAIB 分析发现，在抬起龙骨且未放下任何帆的情况下，游艇在超过60节的风速下就会变得容易侧翻；而当下沉降流（downburst）袭来时，这一阈值几秒钟内就被突破。与“贝叶斯”号同系列的其他游艇都是两根桅杆，而非一根。这一问题本可能更早被发现，若不是另一个因素：超豪华私人游艇并不必遵守与商用船舶同样严格的安全规则。国际安全管理规则（ISM Code）对以私人用途注册的船只并非强制执行。

paragraphs.en: When the downburst struck at 4:06 am, hurricane-force winds exceeding 70 knots hit the Bayesian’s towering mast like a lever, causing the yacht to heel hard to starboard. The vessel capsized within 15 seconds and sank in just 18 minutes, settling 50 meters below on the seabed off Porticello, Sicily.
paragraphs.zh: 当下沉降流在凌晨4:06击中“贝叶斯”号时，超过70节的飓风级风暴掠过这根高耸的主桅，如同杠杆般撬动船身，迫使游艇猛烈向右舷侧倾。船只在15秒内翻覆，并在仅18分钟后沉没，最终在西西里岛波提切洛（Porticello）外海海底沉入约50米深处。

paragraphs.en: The $40 million Bayesian featured a 72-meter aluminum mast—one of the world’s tallest—that gave the 56-meter vessel her elegant profile but created a critical vulnerability. With the keel raised and no sails set, the yacht became vulnerable to capsizing in winds exceeding 60 knots, a threshold crossed when the downburst struck.
paragraphs.zh: 这艘价值4000万美元的“贝叶斯”号装有一根72米高的铝制主桅——这是世界上最高的游艇桅杆之一——它赋予56米长的游艇优雅轮廓，却也造成了致命隐患。在抬起龙骨且未放下帆时，游艇会在风速超过60节时变得容易侧翻，而这一下沉降流来袭时这一临界值已被突破。

paragraphs.en: What are the odds of all this happening? As I spoke to people about Lynch, I became obsessed with the probability of all these untethered events. I tried to find some way to quantify this. Consider his trajectory: Perhaps only one in 500 working-class boys win elite scholarships; one in 10 of those reach Oxbridge; one in 12 of those earn PhDs; one in 10,000 of those become centimillionaires—multiply it out and you’re already at one in 600 million. White-collar defendants are either acquitted or dismissed about 15 percent of the time, but when the CFO is already convicted? Lynch was one of the few CEOs in the past 25 years to walk free from a criminal conviction after his other subordinates had already been convicted. Then that final night: Severe downbursts strike Sicilian anchorages perhaps once every 14 years, but for one just 75 meters wide to bull’s-eye a 56-meter yacht while missing the schooner 100 meters away? Probably on the order of once in 2,300 years. And Stephen Chamberlain—a jogger’s daily risk of fatal collision is about one in 9 million; multiply by Lynch’s yacht-disaster probability and you get roughly one in 760 billion for both dying the same day.
paragraphs.zh: 这种事同时发生的几率到底有多大？在与人讨论林奇时，我反复琢磨这些看似毫无关联事件的概率，试着用数字把它量化。先看他的轨迹：也许每500个工薪阶层男孩里只有1人能拿到顶级奖学金；其中10人里就有1人能进牛津或剑桥；那其中12人里就有1人拿到博士；而其中1万名里就有1人能成为亿万富翁——把这些乘起来，已经是约6亿分之一了。白领被告通常有约15%的概率被判无罪或被驳回，但当财务总监已被定罪时？林奇是过去25年里为数不多的——在其他下属先后被定罪后，仍能从刑事定罪中“走出去”的CEO之一。然后是那最后一夜：在西西里锚地，严重下击暴流大约每14年发生一次；但要恰好在75米宽的范围内正中一艘56米长的游艇，而又错过离它仅100米的另一艘双桅船，概率可能只有约2300年一次。至于斯蒂芬·张伯林，一名慢跑者当日遭遇致命撞击的风险约是900万分之一；再乘以林奇那场游艇灾难的概率，双方同日身亡的概率大约是7600亿分之一。

paragraphs.en: But if you look at things from a different angle, not everything seems up to chance. Lynch was an unruly thinker from his college days and a perfectionist boss who pushed everyone around him to their limits; was it inevitable that he would end up in court eventually, defending his risky decisions? In the same way, the Bayesian’s flaw is the most obvious thing about it. Standing at 72 meters, the aluminum mast was about as tall as the wingspan of a jumbo jet. The physics were baked into the design. Of all the events in Mike Lynch’s improbable life, his death turned on something that was, actually, quite knowable.
paragraphs.zh: 但如果换个角度看，并非一切都像是随机的结果。林奇从大学时代起就是个难以驯服的思想者，也是个把身边每个人都推到极限的完美主义老板；那么他最终走进法庭，为自己的高风险决策辩护，是否注定会发生？同样地，贝叶斯式的思维偏差，在这件事上是最明显的缺陷。那72米高的铝制桅杆几乎和一架宽体客机的翼展一样高。物理规律早已写进了这艘船的设计。放眼林奇那段不可思议的人生，真正左右他死亡的，并不是玄妙难解之事，而是一件早已可以被预见的东西。

paragraphs.en: —With research from Charlie Barlow and Rania Raj
paragraphs.zh: ——附研究：查理·巴洛与拉尼娅·拉贾
